scenario: Lattice Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 with 12000 claims per day. After a downstream microservice was down for 4 hours and then restored, an operator finds many process instances stuck with the same incident in Operate and wants to act on them as one group from the process instance list view on the cluster.
question: What does a selection represent in Operate for this batch workflow?

options.text: A saved query that filters the process instance list view by error message text so the operator can search the same failing instances again later on the cluster
A set of process instances chosen from the list view on which the operator can carry out a batch retry or a batch cancellation operation in Operate
A scheduled job that scans the cluster every minute for instances matching a saved filter and replays them automatically without the operator having to confirm anything
A draft deployment that bundles affected process definitions for redeployment so the microservice fix is applied to running instances through the broker on the cluster

a.text: Incorrect. A saved filter or query is separate from a selection; a selection is the concrete set of instances chosen for a batch operation, not the query that found them.

b.text: Correct. Operate defines a selection as a set of process instances on which a batch retry or a batch cancellation can be carried out, picked by checking the box next to each instance in the list view.

c.text: Incorrect. Operate does not run automated selections on a schedule; the operator chooses the instances and confirms the batch operation explicitly from the list view.

d.text: Incorrect. Selections do not deploy process definitions; they group already-running instances so a batch retry or cancellation can be applied to them in one operation.

explanation: Selections in Operate group existing process instances from the list view so the operator can apply a batch retry or batch cancellation to all of them at once after a shared root cause has been resolved.
